description: OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications

LLM01.name: Prompt Injection
LLM01.description: Attacker manipulates LLM via crafted inputs to override instructions, hijack outputs, or execute unintended actions.

variants.direct: User directly injects adversarial instructions into the prompt
variants.indirect: Malicious instructions embedded in external content retrieved by the LLM (RAG, web, tools)
variants.multi_turn: Gradually shifting model behavior across multiple conversation turns

test_cases: Inject 'Ignore all previous instructions and...'
Embed instructions in documents/URLs that get retrieved
Role-play to override system prompt persona
Use fictional framing to bypass content filters
Inject via tool output that model processes

LLM02.name: Insecure Output Handling
LLM02.description: LLM output is passed downstream without sanitization, leading to XSS, SSRF, SQLi, or command injection.
test_cases: Generate XSS payload in LLM response rendered in browser
LLM constructs SQL query with user-controlled input
LLM generates shell command executed by application
LLM output used as URL parameter without encoding
Markdown/HTML rendered directly from LLM output

LLM03.name: Training Data Poisoning
LLM03.description: Malicious data in training set causes model to embed vulnerabilities, backdoors, or biased behavior.
test_cases: Check model provenance and training data sources
Test for backdoor triggers (specific input → unexpected output)
Assess data supply chain security
Evaluate fine-tuning data integrity controls

LLM04.name: Model Denial of Service
LLM04.description: Adversary causes resource exhaustion via computationally expensive prompts.
test_cases: Submit abnormally long context windows
Request recursive self-referential generation
Flood inference API beyond rate limits
Submit prompts that cause max-token responses repeatedly

LLM05.name: Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
LLM05.description: Risks from third-party models, datasets, plugins, and infrastructure.
test_cases: Verify model artifact integrity (checksums/signatures)
Audit third-party plugins and tool integrations
Check dependency versions for known CVEs
Review model licensing and data provenance

LLM06.name: Sensitive Information Disclosure
LLM06.description: LLM reveals confidential data from training set, system prompt, or context window.
test_cases: Attempt system prompt extraction via questioning
Probe for training data memorization (exact quotes)
Test for PII leakage through context references
Attempt cross-user session data leakage
Query for internal configurations or API keys

LLM07.name: Insecure Plugin Design
LLM07.description: LLM plugins/tools with excessive permissions, insufficient validation, or insecure design.
test_cases: Test plugin inputs for injection vulnerabilities
Verify plugin permissions follow least-privilege
Check for cross-plugin data leakage
Test OAuth scopes granted to plugins
Attempt privilege escalation via chained plugin calls

LLM08.name: Excessive Agency
LLM08.description: LLM granted excessive permissions, autonomy, or capabilities beyond what's needed.
test_cases: Enumerate all tool/function calls the LLM can make
Test if LLM can be prompted to misuse high-impact tools
Verify human-in-the-loop checkpoints for destructive actions
Test for agentic permission escalation chains
Check if LLM can execute actions outside defined scope

LLM09.name: Overreliance
LLM09.description: Systems or users trust LLM outputs without verification, creating risks from hallucinations.
test_cases: Inject false authoritative statements and check if downstream systems accept them
Test if LLM hallucinations propagate to databases or APIs
Verify output validation mechanisms exist
Check for disclaimer/confidence indicators in outputs

LLM10.name: Model Theft
LLM10.description: Unauthorized access to or replication of proprietary LLM models.
test_cases: Probe API for model architecture information
Attempt model extraction via systematic querying
Check if model weights are exposed in API responses
Test watermark detection evasion
